note: Responsive Favicons is a config-form-driven UI module (one settings form, one config object, two alter hooks, no Drush/plugins). All cases are `recipe` (response-graded), not `execution`.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the Responsive Favicons module, what is the admin path (or route name) to configure the favicons? Give the URL.
evals.expected_output: The settings form is at /admin/config/user-interface/responsive_favicons (route responsive_favicons.admin).

evals.prompt: Which permission gates access to the Responsive Favicons settings form on a Drupal site? Give the exact permission machine string.

Using Responsive Favicons, I want Drupal to stop emitting its default core/theme favicon.ico so it doesn't conflict with my uploaded icons. Which config key on responsive_favicons.settings do I enable, and give a drush command to set it.
evals.expected_output: Set remove_default to 1, e.g. drush cset responsive_favicons.settings remove_default 1 -y.

evals.prompt: After enabling Responsive Favicons, requests to /favicon.ico still aren't served by the module. What one-line change to the Drupal .htaccess is required so the module can handle /favicon.ico?
In a custom Drupal module I want to programmatically modify the favicon link/meta tags that the Responsive Favicons module adds to the page head. Which hook do I implement? Name it.

With Responsive Favicons, instead of uploading a zip I want to point the module at favicon files that already live in my theme directory. Which config value of path_type do I use, and what does the path key then reference?
evals.expected_output: Set path_type to 'path' (internal path mode); path is then a directory relative to DRUPAL_ROOT, e.g. /themes/custom/mytheme/favicons.
